quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 9 Quiz 2
quiz.subtitle: Relationship Between Central Angles and Their Intercepted Arcs
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: A central angle of a circle is defined as:
options: An angle whose vertex is at the center of the circle
An angle whose vertex is on the circle
An angle formed by two chords intersecting on the circle
An angle formed by a tangent and a chord
questions.explanation: A central angle has its vertex at the center of the circle, and its sides are two radii. The measure of a central angle equals the measure of its intercepted arc. For example, if $\angle AOB = 60^\circ$, then arc $AB = 60^\circ$.

questions.question: In a circle, the measure of a central angle is $72^\circ$. What is the measure of its intercepted arc?
questions.explanation: The measure of a central angle equals the measure of its intercepted arc. This is a fundamental relationship: $m\angle AOB = m\widehat{AB}$. If the central angle is $72^\circ$, the intercepted arc is also $72^\circ$.

questions.question: An arc that measures exactly $180^\circ$ is called a:
questions.explanation: A semicircle is an arc that measures exactly $180^\circ$. It is intercepted by a diameter, which forms a central angle of $180^\circ$. A semicircle divides the circle into two equal halves.

questions.question: A minor arc is defined as an arc whose measure is:
questions.explanation: A minor arc measures less than $180^\circ$. It is named by its two endpoints (e.g., $\widehat{AB}$). The measure of a minor arc equals the measure of its central angle. The corresponding major arc would measure $360^\circ$ minus the minor arc measure.

questions.question: A circle has a central angle $\angle AOB = 85^\circ$. What is the measure of the major arc $\widehat{ACB}$ (the arc that goes the long way from $A$ to $B$)?
questions.explanation: The minor arc $\widehat{AB}$ equals the central angle: $85^\circ$. The major arc is the remainder of the circle: $360^\circ - 85^\circ = 275^\circ$. Minor arc + major arc = $360^\circ$, the total measure of a circle.

questions.question: In $\odot O$, points $A$ and $B$ lie on the circle. The major arc $\widehat{AB}$ measures $210^\circ$. What is the measure of the central angle $\angle AOB$?
questions.explanation: The minor arc $\widehat{AB} = 360^\circ - 210^\circ = 150^\circ$. Since the central angle equals the measure of the minor arc, $\angle AOB = 150^\circ$. Note: the central angle always corresponds to the minor arc, unless specified otherwise.

questions.question: A circle is divided into $8$ equal arcs by $8$ equally spaced points on the circumference. What is the measure of the central angle that intercepts one of these arcs?
questions.explanation: The total measure of a circle is $360^\circ$. With $8$ equal arcs, each arc measures $\frac{360^\circ}{8} = 45^\circ$. Since the central angle equals its intercepted arc, each central angle also measures $45^\circ$.

questions.question: In $\odot O$, $\angle AOB = 3x + 10$ and the measure of minor arc $\widehat{AB} = 5x - 6$. Find the value of $x$.
questions.explanation: The central angle equals the measure of its intercepted arc: $3x + 10 = 5x - 6$. Solving: $10 + 6 = 5x - 3x$, so $16 = 2x$ and $x = 8$. Check: $\angle AOB = 3(8) + 10 = 34^\circ$ and arc $= 5(8) - 6 = 40 - 6 = 34^\circ$.

questions.question: In a circle, central angle $\angle AOB = 120^\circ$. Points $C$ and $D$ lie on the circle such that $\angle COD = 90^\circ$. What is the ratio of the length of arc $\widehat{AB}$ to arc $\widehat{CD}$?
questions.explanation: In a circle, arc length is proportional to the central angle measure. The ratio of arc $\widehat{AB}$ to arc $\widehat{CD}$ equals $120^\circ : 90^\circ = 12:9 = 4:3$. Since arc $\widehat{AB} = 120^\circ$ and arc $\widehat{CD} = 90^\circ$, the ratio is $4:3$.

questions.question: In $\odot O$, minor arc $\widehat{AB} = 40^\circ$, minor arc $\widehat{BC} = 70^\circ$, and minor arc $\widehat{CA} = 250^\circ$. Points $A$, $B$, and $C$ are on the circle. Is this possible? Why or why not?
options: No, because the three arcs must sum to $360^\circ$, but they sum to $360^\circ$ only if some are major arcs
Yes, because any three arcs sum to $360^\circ$
Yes, because the sum of arcs is unrestricted
questions.explanation: The three non-overlapping arcs around a circle determined by three points must sum to $360^\circ$. Here, $40^\circ + 70^\circ + 250^\circ = 360^\circ$, so it is possible — but arc $\widehat{CA}$ at $250^\circ$ is a major arc. The minor arc $\widehat{CA}$ would be $110^\circ$, making the three minor arcs $40^\circ + 70^\circ + 110^\circ = 220^\circ$, which is impossible for a full circle. The problem correctly identifies arcs, with one being major.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the relationship between central angles and their intercepted arcs, and can distinguish between minor arcs, major arcs, and semicircles.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember: (1) central angle measure = minor arc measure, (2) major arc = $360^\circ$ minus minor arc, (3) a semicircle measures exactly $180^\circ$, (4) all arcs around a circle sum to $360^\circ$.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: (1) identifying whether an arc is minor (less than $180^\circ$) or major (greater than $180^\circ$), (2) using the equation $m\angle = m\widehat{\text{arc}}$ for central angles, (3) remembering that the total circle measures $360^\circ$.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A central angle has its vertex at the center. Its measure equals the measure of its intercepted minor arc. Minor arc < $180^\circ$, major arc > $180^\circ$, semicircle = $180^\circ$. All arcs around a circle sum to $360^\circ$.
